item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know how I missed this when it first came out, but this was another great take on 2006. Awesome stuff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: holy shit I wish I could upvote more than once, I did not seen that twist coming. 106 tales freak me out and Treats even made me lose sleep, knowing that he can appear anywhere and anytime. I went into this thinking that it was just going to be another tale like that but I never thought I would laugh at such a grim and frankly just plain terrifying tale. you somehow made it feel real too, and not just a cheap laugh. 2006 is one of my favorite scips on the site and I think this might be my favorite tale. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Amps up the horror of 106 even more so than its original article, much like Treats did, though to a lesser extent than that (but only due to being shorter in form, and sharing the space…) - which would even be worse, a swift death at its corrosive hands, or being taken alive? - and another entry giving us a glimpse at what an improperly contained 2006 might be like. Is that eye-teeth marble statue a reference to anything itself, or a piece of anart prose of your own baking?

Just one typo - got "trick-r-treat" near the end.

item.title: updooted
item.content:encoded: 2006 is a great character, and you use it well. This is now my favorite standalone tale on the site!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh no. My point was simply that despite all the references made and the title, the proper thing was to at least mention the inspiration in the author post.

You might be intimately familiar with Treats and probably assume most of the site is as well. But imagine if someone came across your tale and they had never read Treats. That was the only worry of mine here (and one you've rectified by mentioning it in the author post).

It's clearly an homage, and that's fine.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This turned out pretty awesome, though it needs a light proofing. There's still a couple minor SPaG issues here and there.

Upvoted originally, but settled on a (hesitant) downvote. I've not read Treats in years, and I didn't pick up on the borrowed scene until seeing the comments here, and rereading that article to compare. Seeing this made the work far less impressive, doubly so considering that section - the scenario and characters, are easily interchangeable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Every time 2006 gets close to becoming truly uncontainable is believably terrifying. An ultimately frightening SCP that wants you alive so you can stay permanently scared - that's good old-fashioned nightmare fuel.

It stands to reason that 106 would be frightened by an entity invulnerable to its ichor.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: More of a spiritual-companion-cum-love-letter, similar to the relationship between 231 and 2317. They're not meant to be directly linked, but one is definitely designed to evoke the other.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I assumed the homage was obvious, given all the deliberate nods and the title similarity sirpudding mentioned, but I have updated my author post to reflect this.

The whole angel getting attacked from behind thing is almost word for word from that tale.

In your mind, does this cross the line from "homage" into "plagiarism"? I didn't think so until now. A previous draft referenced the bowling ball analogy and was left with other references on the cutting room floor to avoid this, so I'm curious.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-2006 was nearby, watching as the nearest MTF loaded the old an onto the truck.

Hold up

SCP-2006 was nearby, watching as the nearest MTF loaded the old man onto the truck.

Aaaahhh. Much better

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Presumably that's why it's called Tricks? I think this is supposed to be an immediate sequel?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Probably at the very least mention that some of this was inspired by "Treats". The whole angel getting attacked from behind thing is almost word for word from that tale.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I never imagined that one of the most dangerous SCP's on the site would ever be written convincingly into being defeated, let alone afraid, let alone by one of the least likely SCP's out there. But you did it. You beat 106 with a space gorilla. I love this piece.

I already upvoted no worries

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Too Spooky is one of my absolute favorite entries on the site. I wanna huggle it so hard. Treats is one of my absolute favorite tales, and I took a lot of inspiration from it here as you can probably tell. I played around with the idea of using 2006 to contain or recapture other anomalies in a previous tale, and the Halloween Contest felt like a great place to explore that idea.

Done for the 2018 Halloween Contest.
